text: Managing risk is a key skill in business and daily life. It begins with identifying potential risks that could disrupt your plans, such as financial loss, accidents, or project delays. Once risks are identified, you develop mitigation strategies to reduce their likelihood or impact. For example, you might diversify investments or improve safety procedures. Even with these measures, some risks remain, so it is wise to create contingency plans—backup actions to take if problems arise. Finally, you should continuously monitor the situation, as risks can change over time. By regularly reviewing and adjusting your approach, you can stay prepared and reduce uncertainty.
